subject: Sociology
title: Interactionism in the Classroom
description: A fast breakdown of Interactionism in the Classroom for builders.

hook.text: A teacher labels one student gifted and another troublemaker. Watch what happens next — both live up to it.

buildup.text: Interactionists study micro-level processes: how teacher expectations and labels shape student outcomes.

discovery.text: Rosenthal and Jacobson's study showed that students labeled as bloomers improved — even though labels were random.

twist.text: This is the self-fulfilling prophecy. Expectations create the very reality they claim to merely describe.

climax.text: Classroom interactions are not neutral. Every look, comment, and grade carries power to shape a child's path.

quiz.question: What did Rosenthal and Jacobson's study demonstrate?
options: Teacher expectations can create self-fulfilling prophecies
